索引 审核 图谱 问题
ACK timing before long-running agent work Actions on the wrong target machine or host can cause unsafe operational changes. Agents can mislead users if failures are reported as successful outcomes. Case-sensitive database comparisons can miss logically equivalent values. Changes can introduce regressions when not tested after modification Missing replay data compromises the ability to debug or verify past execution behaviors OpenClaw dispatch routing failures OpenClaw Slack replay and post-deploy stability failures Post-deploy stability smoke test run 'openclaw' resulted in an overall failure OpenClaw dispatch routing failures Stale caches can hide current behavior or serve outdated assets

Actions on the wrong target machine or host can cause unsafe operational changes.

When to Use

Use this procedure before performing any restart, modification, or operational action on a system, server, or host.

Context

Operational actions often involve destructive or disruptive changes such as restarts or configuration mutations. Performing these actions on the wrong target machine can lead to service outages, data loss, or unsafe operational states. Verification of the target identity is a critical safety step.

Procedure

Before initiating any operational action:

1. Pause before executing the command or change. 2. Confirm the target machine or host matches the intended target specified in the operation plan or ticket. 3. Validate that the session context, terminal prompt, or management console is connected to the correct host. 4. Proceed with the action only after confirmation is successful.

Operating Rule

Do not restart or mutate systems before confirming the target.

Verify

Verify the selected target matches the intended machine or host by checking the hostname, IP address, or unique system identifier against the operational instructions.

Reusable Lessons

  • Before restarting, modifying, or operating on a target machine or host, confirm the target machine or host before taking action.
  • Do not restart or mutate systems before confirming the target.

Agent Use

  • **Use this page when:** An agent is about to execute a shell command, API call, or configuration change that affects system state (restart, stop, modify).
  • **Apply it by:** Interrogating the current environment (e.g., running `hostname`, checking environment variables, or verifying session metadata) to ensure it aligns with the requested target parameters provided in the task prompt.
  • **Verify by:** Cross-referencing the discovered environment details with the target details in the user prompt or task definition before emitting the final tool call or command.
  • **Do not use it when:** Performing read-only operations that do not alter state or target machines, or when the operation is explicitly context-agnostic (e.g., local temporary resource generation).
